mechanism: Inhibits multiple receptor tyrosine kinases to reduce proliferative signaling
Inhibits SRC-family kinases and ABL to block downstream signaling
Inhibits multiple kinases; FLT3 activity reported in some contexts
additional_notes: Keep low DMSO; note states FLT3 activity reported in some contexts

mechanism: Inhibits EGFR signaling; noted as often acting via off-target stress mechanisms in AML
additional_notes: Many AML lines need higher µM

mechanism: Inhibits MEK to suppress MAPK/ERK pathway signaling
Inhibits mTORC1 to reduce growth and protein synthesis signaling (often cytostatic)
Inhibits the proteasome, causing accumulation of ubiquitinated proteins and apoptosis
additional_notes: Very potent; avoid plastic binding issues

mechanism: Inhibits the proteasome, promoting proteotoxic stress and apoptosis
additional_notes: Short half-life in media; timing matters

mechanism: Inhibits BCL-2 and BCL-XL to induce apoptosis
additional_notes: Can be more cytotoxic; platelet toxicity irrelevant in vitro

mechanism: Inhibits JAK1 and JAK2 to reduce cytokine signaling
Inhibits JAK family kinases to reduce cytokine signaling
additional_notes: Typically weaker than ruxolitinib in AML

mechanism: Inhibits histone deacetylases to alter chromatin acetylation and gene expression
Weakly inhibits HDACs, affecting chromatin acetylation
additional_notes: Needs mM-range in vitro; use sodium valproate (aqueous)

category: Mitochondrial complex I inhibitor (metabolic stress agent)
mechanism: Induces metabolic stress by inhibiting mitochondrial complex I
additional_notes: Needs mM-range in vitro; media glucose strongly affects response
